code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase.
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is the leader of",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "is the language of",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Predicates that need inverted wording (object first)
    invert_predicates = {
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the language of {s}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as currency",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The main ethnic group in {s} is {o}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the hometown of {s}",
    }

    def clean(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"').strip("'")

    clauses = []
    last_subject = None

    for tr in triples:
        subj = tr.subject
        pred = tr.predicate
        obj = clean(tr.object)

        # Use inverted phrasing when appropriate
        if pred in invert_predicates:
            clause = invert_predicates[pred](subj, obj)
        else:
            verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
            # Special handling for count predicates to add unit word
            if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{subj} {verb} {obj} floors"
            else:
                clause = f"{subj} {verb} {obj}"

        # If the subject is the same as the previous one, omit repeating it
        if subj == last_subject and pred not in invert_predicates:
            # drop the subject, keep only verb phrase
            clause = clause.replace(f"{subj} ", "", 1)
        clauses.append(clause)
        last_subject = subj

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""

    # Insert commas and a final period
    sentence = ", ".join(clauses) + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
